relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Priestly consecration begins with complete offerings.
Unleavened bread and oil prepare the basket.
Bread and animals are brought near.
Aaron and sons are washed at the tent entrance.
Aaron is clothed in holy garments.
The holy crown rests on the turban.
Oil pours over Aaron's head.
His sons are clothed.
Their hands are filled for priesthood.
Hands are laid on the bull.
The offering is made before YHWH.
Blood/life marks altar horns and base.
Inner portions rise as smoke.
The sin offering is burned outside camp.
Hands are laid on the first ram.
Blood/life is thrown around the altar.
The ram is prepared in pieces.
The whole ram rises as offering to YHWH.
Hands are laid on the second ram.
Ear, thumb, and toe are marked for holy hearing, doing, and walking.
Blood/life and oil sanctify people and garments.
The ram of filling gives its portions.
Bread is taken from the basket before YHWH.
All is placed on priestly palms and waved.
The offering rises as soothing scent.
The breast is waved as portion.
Wave breast and contribution thigh are sanctified.
Priestly portions come from peace offerings.
Aaron's garments pass to sons after him.
Seven days the succeeding priest wears them.
The ram is boiled in holy place.
Aaron and sons eat at the tent entrance.
They eat what consecrates them.
Holy remnants are burned by morning.
Seven days fill their hands.
The altar is cleansed and sanctified.
The altar becomes Holy of Holies in contact.
Two lambs are offered daily.
Morning and evening rhythm begin.
Grain, oil, and wine accompany the lamb.
Evening offering mirrors morning offering.
The continual offering marks the meeting place where YHWH speaks.
YHWH meets Israel and sanctifies by glory.
Tent, altar, Aaron, and sons are sanctified for service.
YHWH dwells in Israel's midst and becomes their Divine.
They know YHWH as the One who brought them out to dwell among them.

contemplative_note: Exodus 29 turns priesthood into consecrated hearing, hand, and walk. The whole purpose returns at the end: liberation so Divine presence may dwell in the midst.
